verse_specific_anchors: puṣpitā vāc — flowery / blossomed-tree speech (the visual-beauty-without-substance image)
avipaścit — the undiscerning (the scoping locus of the critique — speaker, not text)
veda-vāda-rata — engrossed in the (kāmya-karma) utterances (specifically scoped to a portion)
na anyad asti — the closure clause 'there is nothing else' as the diagnostic
verdict_reason: The engineering analog hangs on the specific 'there-is-nothing-else' closure-clause diagnostic, which is unique to 2.42. Could it come from 2.43? No — 2.43 supplies the predicative content (which fruits, which rituals) without the closure framing. 2.44? No — 2.44 names the consequence (resolved buddhi cannot establish). 2.45? No — 2.45 gives the operative imperative. The 'flowery/in-bloom speech with implicit closure clause' framing belongs specifically to 2.42. PASS for both 7.6 and 3.5.
